pattern: Every road only ever points to a larger city index, so the graph stays a DAG in index order and one forward relaxation pass after each new road recomputes the shortest distance to the last city.
hints: Every road goes from a smaller city index to a larger one, so processing cities in index order 0..n-1 is always a valid topological order — there is never a cycle to worry about.

After adding a query's road, redo a single forward pass: keep a running best-distance array, and whenever you reach a city, relax every road leaving it (the default road to the next city, plus any added roads) into the destination's best distance.
n and the number of queries are both small here, so simply recomputing the whole forward pass from scratch after each query is fast enough — no incremental bookkeeping is required.

edge_inputs.description: single query shortcut spans most of the path
two disjoint shortcuts stack their savings
minimum city count with the only legal query
a single query covering the whole path
overlapping queries must combine correctly, not just take the smaller span
a later query re-shortcuts the whole path
a chain of small shortcuts laid end to end

stress.description: random distinct legal queries over a moderate number of cities
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(3, min(size, 500))
    q_count = max(1, min(size, 500))
    seen = set()
    queries = []
    attempts = 0
    while len(queries) < q_count and attempts < q_count * 20:
        attempts += 1
        u = rng.randint(0, n - 3)
        v = rng.randint(u + 2, n - 1)
        if (u, v) in seen:
            continue
        seen.add((u, v))
        queries.append([u, v])
    if not queries:
        queries = [[0, n - 1]]
    return [n, queries]
